title: Quadratic Equation and Inequalities - Chapter Test

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha_1,\alpha_2,....,\alpha_7$$ be the roots of the equation $${x^7} + 3{x^5} - 13{x^3} - 15x = 0$$ and $$|{\alpha _1}| \ge |{\alpha _2}| \ge \,...\, \ge \,|{\alpha _7}|$$. Then $$\alpha_1\alpha_2-\alpha_3\alpha_4+\alpha_5\alpha_6$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let f(x) be a quadratic polynomial such that f($$-$$2) + f(3) = 0. If one of the roots of f(x) = 0 is $$-$$1, then the sum of the roots of f(x) = 0 is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta$$ are the roots of the equation $$x^{4}+x^{3}+x^{2}+x+1=0$$, then $$\alpha^{2021}+\beta^{2021}+\gamma^{2021}+\delta^{2021}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: For x $$\in$$ R, the number of real roots of the equation $$3{x^2} - 4\left| {{x^2} - 1} \right| + x - 1 = 0$$ is ________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:

$${x^4} - 3{x^3} - 2{x^2} + 3x + 1 = 0$$ is _________.

questions.questionText: The numbers of pairs (a, b) of real numbers, such that whenever $$\alpha$$ is a root of the equation x<sup>2</sup> + ax + b = 0, $$\alpha$$<sup>2</sup> $$-$$ 2 is also a root of this equation, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the quadratic equation $(\lambda+2) x^2-3 \lambda x+4 \lambda=0, \lambda \neq-2$, has two positive roots, then the number of possible integral values of $\lambda$ is :

questions.questionText: Let $$\lambda \ne 0$$ be a real number. Let $$\alpha,\beta$$ be the roots of the equation $$14{x^2} - 31x + 3\lambda = 0$$ and $$\alpha,\gamma$$ be the roots of the equation $$35{x^2} - 53x + 4\lambda = 0$$. Then $${{3\alpha } \over \beta }$$ and $${{4\alpha } \over \gamma }$$ are the roots of the equation
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The sum of all integral values of k (k $$\ne$$ 0) for which the equation $${2 \over {x - 1}} - {1 \over {x - 2}} = {2 \over k}$$ in x has no real roots, is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The sum of all real values of $$x$$ for which $$\frac{3 x^{2}-9 x+17}{x^{2}+3 x+10}=\frac{5 x^{2}-7 x+19}{3 x^{2}+5 x+12}$$ is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The set of all $$a \in \mathbb{R}$$ for which the equation $$x|x-1|+|x+2|+a=0$$ has exactly one real root, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\mathbf{S}=\left\{x \in \mathbf{R}:(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2})^x+(\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2})^x=10\right\}$. Then the number of elements in $\mathrm{S}$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

$${\alpha \over {1 - {\alpha ^2}}} + {\beta \over {1 - {\beta ^2}}}$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText:

questions.questionText:
If $$\alpha, \beta$$ are the roots of the equation

$$ x^{2}-\left(5+3^{\sqrt{\log _{3} 5}}-5^{\sqrt{\log _{5} 3}}\right)x+3\left(3^{\left(\log _{3} 5\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}-5^{\left(\log _{5} 3\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}}-1\right)=0 $$,

then the equation, whose roots are $$\alpha+\frac{1}{\beta}$$ and $$\beta+\frac{1}{\alpha}$$, is :

questions.questionText: The number of real roots of the equation $$\sqrt{x^{2}-4 x+3}+\sqrt{x^{2}-9}=\sqrt{4 x^{2}-14 x+6}$$, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The equation $\mathrm{e}^{4 x}+8 \mathrm{e}^{3 x}+13 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}-8 \mathrm{e}^{x}+1=0, x \in \mathbb{R}$ has :
options: (A) two solutions and both are negative
(B) two solutions and only one of them is negative
(C) four solutions two of which are negative

questions.questionText: The number of distinct real roots of the equation $$|x+1||x+3|-4|x+2|+5=0$$, is _______
